name: Twice-Baked Potatoes
recipeInstructions:
1. Scrub baking potatoes with a brush.
2. Prick them several times with a fork.
3. Place the potatoes in a shallow baking dish.
4. Micro-cook, uncovered, on 100% power for 6 to 8 minutes or until tender.
5. Cut a lengthwise slice from the top of each potato.
6. Discard the skin and place the potato portions from the top slices in a mixing bowl.
7. Scoop out the insides of the potatoes, leaving two 1/4-inch thick shells; set aside.
8. Add the potato insides to the mixing bowl containing the potato portions from the top slices.
9. Mash everything together.
10. Stir in shredded cheddar cheese, butter or margarine, and sliced green onion.
11. Beat in enough milk until the mixture is stiff but still creamy.
12. Season with salt and freshly ground pepper to taste.
13. Pile the mashed potato mixture into the reserved potato shells.
14. Return the dish to the oven.
15. Micro-cook, uncovered, on 100% power for 2 to 3 minutes or until heated through.
16. Sprinkle with paprika, if desired.
